title: Microsoft raises Xbox Series console prices by up to $150 in August
summary: The increase, attributed to the component shortage and rising costs, affects all models and eliminates the 2 TB version.

title: Apple increases its device prices due to rising RAM costs
summary: The company applies increases to Mac, iPad, and other products due to the rising cost of NAND Flash and DRAM chips.

title: Alternatives to the Steam Machine emerge to compete with its high price
summary: A French retailer presents a mock PC called 'Stim Machine' and DIY projects made with 3D printers appear.

title: IBM reveals sub-1 nanometer transistor technology for future chips
summary: The new vertical stacking method allows further progress in processor miniaturization.

title: Tim Sweeney criticizes Valve for labeling games with generative AI
summary: The CEO of Epic Games calls Steam's decision to include labels about AI-generated content 'irresponsible'.

title: Valve scales back performance promises for the Steam Machine
summary: The company changes the claim of 4K at 60 FPS to a vaguer 'up to 4K'.

title: AMD relaunches the Ryzen 7 5800X3D in a 10th anniversary special edition
summary: The processor returns to the market with limited stock that is already being hoarded by resellers.

title: ASUS brings back TSME encryption to its AM5 motherboards with a beta BIOS
summary: The company responds to the controversy and restores security on its motherboards for Ryzen 9000 processors.

title: AceMagic launches a promotion with up to 20% discount on its mini PCs
summary: The systems with AMD Ryzen are available at reduced prices through an exclusive limited-time coupon.

title: GTA VI: rumors of censorship and possible disc release in December
summary: Conflicting information circulates about the game's content and its physical format.

title: Windows 10 extends its free support until 2027
summary: Users will be able to continue updating the operating system at no extra cost for three more years.

title: Sony confirms massive layoffs at Bungie
summary: The video game studio suffers a significant staff cut as part of Sony's restructuring.

title: Riot removes the permanent execution requirement for its Vanguard anti-cheat
summary: The new on-demand mode requires a strict security configuration in Windows 11.

title: The AI token boom deflates due to high costs
summary: An audio leak from a consulting firm reveals that no one knows how to measure AI effectiveness.

title: Apple would skip M6 Pro and M6 Max chips to go directly to M7
summary: Apple's upcoming processors might skip a generation according to supply chain reports.

title: Morgan Stanley forecasts that AMD EPYC Venice will surpass NVIDIA Vera CPUs in volume by 2027
summary: Analysts predict significant growth for EPYC processors compared to NVIDIA's offerings.

title: Intel Core Ultra 9 285K drops to $445 on Amazon
summary: Intel's top-of-the-line processor significantly reduces its price on the sales platform.

title: Framework adopts ADATA XPG MARS 970 SSDs and warns of upcoming CPU price increases
summary: The modular laptop company updates its components and alerts about future increases.

title: Enshrouded announces full release and PS5 date after two years in early access
summary: The survival action game leaves Early Access with a version 1.0 on all platforms.

title: Half-Life 2 can now be played in the browser thanks to an unofficial WebGL 2 version
summary: A community project allows enjoying Valve's classic without needing to download anything.
